channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Airlines suspend Middle East flights amid escalating tensions following US-Israel strikes on Iran
item.description: Several airlines have cancelled or rerouted flights to the Middle East after deadly strikes on Iran by the US and Israel. British Airways, Virgin Atlantic, and Wizz Air are among those scaling back th...

item.title: Houston Cougars Favored Against Kansas Jayhawks in Big 12 Matchup
item.description: The Houston Cougars are set to face the Kansas Jayhawks in Lawrence, KS, with tip-off scheduled for 9 p.m. ET on ESPN. Houston is slightly favored by 2 points and has a moneyline of -135, while the ga...

item.title: Zack Snyder defends his fanbase and addresses criticism of Batman v Superman
item.description: Zack Snyder, director of several DC films including 'Man of Steel' and 'Batman v Superman: Dawn of Justice,' has responded to long-standing criticisms of his fanbase and defended the controversial 'Ba...

item.title: After Cartel Rampage, Puerto Vallarta Works to Restore Calm Amid Visible Damage
item.description: The BBC reports from Puerto Vallarta, a popular coastal resort town in Mexico’s Jalisco state, following a wave of cartel-related violence that left visible destruction across parts of the city. Journ...

item.title: Senator Calls for Investigation into FBI Director’s Private Jet Use Amid Claims of Delayed Responses
item.description: A senior Democratic senator has called for an investigation into FBI Director Kash Patel over allegations that his personal use of the bureau’s private aircraft delayed responses to major criminal cas...

item.title: Shabana Mahmood urges Labour support for tougher asylum laws to prevent far-right immigration policies
item.description: Home Secretary Shabana Mahmood is set to introduce new legislation to overhaul the UK's asylum system, adopting a tougher approach to migration. Mahmood argues that the current asylum system is too ge...

item.title: Brent Council Enforcement Officers Tackle Persistent Fly-Tipping in London Borough
item.description: The London Borough of Brent has been highlighted as England's fly-tipping capital, recording around 35,000 incidents in a recent year, leading to widespread rubbish accumulation including fridges, sof...

item.title: A DNA test revealed unexpected family secrets and changed my life forever
item.description: In this deeply personal story, a woman recounts how a simple DNA test led her to uncover hidden family secrets. Initially seeking to learn more about her father's unknown origins, she was shocked to d...

item.title: US avoids commemorating war anniversary; 'Prince of Darkness' comment noted
item.description: The BBC article mentions that the United States has chosen not to observe a specific war anniversary, reflecting current political or societal stances regarding historical military events. Additionall...

item.title: Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor's shopping deliveries face reluctance from Waitrose drivers
item.description: Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor, the disgraced former Duke of York, is reportedly causing a headache for Waitrose delivery drivers. According to sources, staff at the supermarket chain have been 'drawing s...

item.title: Security Flaw in Robot Vacuums Exposes Privacy Risks for 6,700 Users
item.description: A security vulnerability in robot vacuums equipped with cameras and microphones has raised significant privacy concerns. Sammy Azdoufal, a robot vacuum owner, discovered that by experimenting with his...

item.title: Keir Starmer Chairs Emergency Cobra Meeting Amid Rising Tensions in Iran
item.description: Keir Starmer, the UK's Prime Minister, chaired a Cobra meeting in response to the escalating tensions between the US, Israel, and Iran. The emergency meeting was convened after airstrikes by the US an...

item.title: Richard Engel Describes Devastation and Limited Access in Post-Ceasefire Gaza During IDF-Guided Visit
item.description: Four months after a ceasefire agreement ended intense fighting between Israel and Hamas in Gaza, Sky News correspondent Richard Engel provides an update on the situation in the enclave. In a podcast d...

item.title: Iran retaliates with strikes on US and Israeli military bases across the Middle East
item.description: In the wake of co-ordinated airstrikes by the US and Israel on Iranian military targets, Tehran has launched a series of retaliatory strikes across the Middle East. Explosions were reported in key reg...

item.title: Ancient chemical evidence points to sea sponges as Earth's first animals
item.description: A study from the Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT) has uncovered chemical evidence suggesting that the first animals on Earth were likely ancient sea sponges. The team found molecular traces...

item.title: Government Proposes Changes to SEND System in England Amid Parental Concerns
item.description: The UK government has released its Schools White Paper, outlining major reforms to the Special Educational Needs and Disabilities (SEND) system in England. Starting in September 2029, children's eligi...

item.title: Bodo/Glimt Reach Champions League Last 16 After Historic Aggregate Win Over Inter Milan
item.description: Bodo/Glimt produced one of the most remarkable results in recent Champions League history by eliminating Inter Milan with a 5-2 aggregate victory to reach the last 16 for the first time. Holding a 3-1...

item.title: UK Energy Bills Set to Decrease in April Following Government Policy Changes
item.description: Energy bills in England, Wales, and Scotland are set to decrease in April due to government changes in policy costs. The upcoming reduction, expected to be announced by Ofgem, will affect nearly all h...

item.title: Guide to Watching the Total Lunar Eclipse on March 3, 2026
item.description: On March 3, 2026, a total lunar eclipse, often called a ‘blood moon,’ will take place. This rare event occurs when the Earth aligns between the Sun and the Moon, casting a reddish hue across the lunar...

item.title: Tourists in Dubai react to Iranian missile interception during Middle East retaliation
item.description: Tourists in Dubai were caught in a tense moment as Iranian missiles streaked overhead in retaliation for US and Israeli airstrikes targeting Iran's Supreme Leader, Ayatollah Khamenei. A video posted o...
